have h₁ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), ∑ (e : p.edgeSet), p.edgeCount e = p.length := by
    intro p
    induction p with
    | nil =>
      simp [G.LoopWalk.support, G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount]
    | cons h p ih =>
      simp [G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.length, G.LoopWalk.edgeSet, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton] at ih ⊢
      <;>
      (try { aesop }) <;>
      (try {
        cases p with
        | nil => simp [h]
        | cons h₁ p₁ => simp_all [G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.length, G.LoopWalk.edgeSet, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Sym2.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
        })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Sym2.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try { aesop })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.length, G.LoopWalk.edgeSet, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
        <;>
        (try { aesop })
      })
      <;>
      simp_all [Sym2.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
      <;>
      aesop
    | loop p ih =>
      simp [G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.length, G.LoopWalk.edgeSet, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton] at ih ⊢
      <;>
      (try { aesop }) <;>
      (try {
        cases p with
        | nil => contradiction
        | cons h₁ p₁ => simp_all [G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.length, G.LoopWalk.edgeSet, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
        <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          simp_all [Sym2.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
          <;>
          (try { aesop })
        })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [Sym2.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
        <;>
        (try { aesop })
      }) <;>
      (try {
        simp_all [G.LoopWalk.edges, G.LoopWalk.length, G.LoopWalk.edgeSet, G.LoopWalk.edgeCount, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
        <;>
        (try { aesop })
      })
      <;>
      simp_all [Sym2.ext_iff, Prod.ext_iff]
      <;>
      aesop
  have h₂ := h₁ p
  simpa using h₂

        Finset.sum_map, Finset.sum_const, ring]

        continue, countP_map, Finset.sum_map, Finset.sum_const, ring])
      <;>
      (try

          <;>
          aesop
        })

have h₁ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), ∑(e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = p.length := by
    intro p
    have h₂ : ∑(e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = p.edges.length := by
      calc
        _ = ∑(e : edgeSet p), countP (· = e) p.edges := rfl
        _ = ∑(e : edgeSet p), countP (· = e) p.edges := rfl
        _ = p.edges.length := by
          have h₃ : ∑(e : (p.edges).toFinset), countP (· = e) p.edges = p.edges.length := by
            rw [Finset.sum_countP_eq_card]
            <;> simp
          simpa [edgeSet] using h₃
    have h₃ : p.edges.length = p.length := by
      have h₄ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), p.edges.length = p.length := by
        intro p
        induction p with
        | nil => simp [edges, darts, length]
        | cons h p ih =>
          simp_all [edges, darts, length, List.map_cons, List.length]
          <;> try simp_all [Sym2.mk.injEq]
          <;> try aesop
        | loop p ih =>
          simp_all [edges, darts, length, List.map_cons, List.length]
          <;> try simp_all [Sym2.mk.injEq]
          <;> try aesop
      exact h₄ p
    calc
      _ = p.edges.length := by rw [h₂]
      _ = p.length := by rw [h₃]
  exact h₁ p

Actually, perhaps it's easier to use the fact that:
`∑ x in L.toFinset, L.count x = ∑ x : X, if x ∈ L.toFinset then L.count x else 0 = ∑ x : X, L.count x = L.sum (fun x => 1) = L.length`.

But we don't have the type `X`, but we can work in Lean with `Multiset`. 

Alternatively, perhaps use `List.countP` to see that `∑ e in L.toFinset, L.count e = L.length` because for each element in `L`, it appears in exactly one term of the sum (`e ∈ L.toFinset`).

But perhaps a better approach is to realize that for every element in the list `L`, there is exactly one corresponding term in the sum (the term for its value) where it contributes 1. 

But perhaps not in Lean. 

However, we can prove that `(L.toFinset).sum (fun x => L.count x) = L.length` as follows:
For any `x`, it appears in `L` exactly `L.count x` times, and thus for the sum over the distinct elements of `L`, each occurrence in `L` is counted exactly once (in the term corresponding to its value). 

Thus, the sum is `L.length`.

But to formalize this in Lean, perhaps use `Multiset` and `count`. 

Alternatively, perhaps better, prove `∑ e in p.edges.toFinset, p.edges.count e = p.edges.length`, and then note that `p.edges.toFinset = p.edgeSet`.

    ∑ (e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = ∑ (e : edgeSet p), countP (· = e) p.edges := by rfl
    _ = ∑ (e : edgeSet p), 1 := by
      -- Since each edge in edgeSet p appears exactly once in edges p, the count is 1.
      have h : ∀ (e : Sym2 V), e ∈ (edgeSet p : Finset (Sym2 V)) → countP (· = e) p.edges = 1 := by
        intro e he
        have h₁ : e ∈ (p.edges.toFinset : Finset (Sym2 V)) := by
          simpa [edgeSet] using he
        have h₂ : countP (· = e) p.edges = 1 := by
          have h₃ : countP (· = e) p.edges = if e ∈ (p.edges.toFinset : Finset (Sym2 V)) then 1 else 0 := by
            -- Since edges p is a list, and e is in the toFinset of edges p, the count is 1.
            simp [countP_eq_of_mem, List.toFinset_eq, Finset.mem_coe, Finset.mem_singleton]
            <;>
            (try contradiction) <;>
            (try simp_all) <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
          split_ifs at h₃ <;> simp_all
          <;>
          (try contradiction) <;>
          (try simp_all) <;>
          (try omega) <;>
          (try aesop)
        exact h₂
      -- Summing over all edges in edgeSet p, each term is 1.
      have h₂ : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), countP (· = e) p.edges = ∑ (e : edgeSet p), 1 := by
        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
        intro e he
        exact h e he
      rw [h₂]
    _ = p.edges.length := by
      -- The sum of 1 over all edges in edgeSet p is the cardinality of edgeSet p.
      have h : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), 1 = (edgeSet p : Finset (Sym2 V)).card := by
        simp [Finset.sum_const]
      rw [h]
      -- The cardinality of edgeSet p is the length of edges p.
      have h₂ : (edgeSet p : Finset (Sym2 V)).card = p.edges.length := by
        simp [edgeSet]
        <;>
        (try contradiction) <;>
        (try simp_all) <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try aesop)
      rw [h₂]
    _ = p.length := by
      -- The length of edges p is the length of p.
      have h : p.edges.length = p.length := by
        -- Prove by induction on p that the length of edges p is the length of p.
        have h₃ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), p.edges.length = p.length := by
          intro p
          induction p with
          | nil => simp
          | cons h p ih =>
            simp_all [edges, darts, length, List.length]
            <;>
            (try contradiction) <;>
            (try simp_all) <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
          | loop p ih =>
            simp_all [edges, darts, length, List.length]
            <;>
            (try contradiction) <;>
            (try simp_all) <;>
            (try omega) <;>
            (try aesop)
        exact h₃ p
      rw [h]

have h₁ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), ∑ (e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = p.length := by
    intro p
    have h₂ : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = ∑ (e : edgeSet p), (countP (· = e) p.edges) := rfl
    rw [h₂]
    -- Use the fact that the sum of the counts of edges is equal to the length of the walk
    have h₃ : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), (countP (· = e) p.edges) = p.edges.length := by
      -- Use the property of the countP function to count the number of occurrences of each edge in the list
      have h₄ : ∑ (e : (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset), (countP (· = e) p.edges) = p.edges.length := by
        -- Use the lemma that the sum of counts of elements in a list equals the length of the list
        rw [List.sum_toFinset_countP_eq_length]
        <;> simp [Function.funext_iff]
        <;> aesop
      -- The edgeSet p is the same as (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset
      have h₅ : edgeSet p = (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset := by
        rfl
      rw [h₅]
      -- Use the previous result to conclude the proof
      exact h₄
    -- The length of the edges list is the same as the length of the walk
    have h₆ : p.edges.length = p.length := by
      -- Prove that the length of the edges list is the same as the length of the walk by induction on p
      have h₇ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), p.edges.length = p.length := by
        intro p
        induction p with
        | nil => simp [edges, length]
        | cons _ _ u v' h p ih =>
          simp_all [edges, length, List.length_cons, List.map_cons]
          <;>
          simp_all [List.length_cons, List.map_cons]
          <;>
          ring_nf at *
          <;>
          omega
        | loop u v p ih =>
          simp_all [edges, length, List.length_cons, List.map_cons]
          <;>
          simp_all [List.length_cons, List.map_cons]
          <;>
          ring_nf at *
          <;>
          omega
      -- Apply the general result to the specific p
      exact h₇ p
    -- Combine the previous results to get the final result
    linarith
  -- Apply the general result to the specific p
  exact h₁ p

have h₁ : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = ∑ (e : edgeSet p), (p.edges.count e : ℕ) := by
    apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
    intro e _
    rw [show edgeCount p e = (p.edges.count e : ℕ) by rfl]
  rw [h₁]
  have h₂ : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), (p.edges.count e : ℕ) = (p.edges.length : ℕ) := by
    have h₃ : ∑ (e : (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset), (p.edges.count e : ℕ) = (p.edges.length : ℕ) := by
      calc
        ∑ (e : (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset), (p.edges.count e : ℕ) = ∑ (e : (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset), (p.edges.count e : ℕ) := rfl
        _ = (p.edges.length : ℕ) := by
          have h₄ : ∀ (l : List (Sym2 V)), ∑ (e : l.toFinset), (l.count e : ℕ) = l.length := by
            intro l
            induction l with
            | nil => simp
            | cons x xs ih =>
              simp_all [List.count_cons, List.toFinset_cons, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
              <;>
              (try omega) <;>
              (try simp_all [List.count_cons, Finset.sum_singleton, Finset.mem_singleton]) <;>
              (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
              <;>
              (try {
                aesop
              })
              <;>
              (try {
                simp_all [List.count_cons, Finset.sum_singleton, Finset.mem_singleton]
                <;> omega
              })
              <;>
              (try {
                simp_all [List.count_cons, Finset.sum_insert, Finset.mem_singleton]
                <;> omega
              })
          have h₆ := h₄ (p.edges)
          exact h₆
    have h₇ : edgeSet p = (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)).toFinset := by
      rfl
    rw [h₇] at *
    exact h₃
  rw [h₂]
  have h₃ : (p.edges.length : ℕ) = p.length := by
    have h₄ : p.edges.length = p.length := by
      induction p with
      | nil => rfl
      | cons h p ih =>
        simp_all [edges, length, List.map, List.cons, List.append]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
        <;>
        (try aesop)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [edges, length, List.map, List.cons, List.append])
        <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
      | loop p ih =>
        simp_all [edges, length, List.map, List.cons, List.append]
        <;>
        (try omega) <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
        <;>
        (try aesop)
        <;>
        (try simp_all [edges, length, List.map, List.cons, List.append])
        <;>
        (try ring_nf at * <;> omega)
    exact_mod_cast h₄
  rw [h₃]
  <;>
  norm_cast

have h₁ : ∑ e : edgeSet p, edgeCount p e = (p.edges.length : ℕ) := by
    have h₂ : ∀ (l : List (Sym2 V)) [DecidableEq (Sym2 V)], ∑ e in l.toFinset, (l.count e : ℕ) = l.length := by
      intro l _
      have h₃ : ∀ (e : Sym2 V), e ∈ l.toFinset → (l.count e : ℕ) = l.count e := by
        intro e _
        rfl
      calc
        ∑ e in l.toFinset, (l.count e : ℕ) = ∑ e in l.toFinset, (l.count e : ℕ) := rfl
        _ = ∑ e in l.toFinset, (l.count e : ℕ) := rfl
        _ = l.length := by
          calc
            ∑ e in l.toFinset, (l.count e : ℕ) = ∑ e in l.toFinset, (l.count e : ℕ) := rfl
            _ = (l.toFinset.sum (fun e => l.count e) : ℕ) := rfl
            _ = l.length := by
              have h₄ : (l.toFinset.sum (fun e => l.count e) : ℕ) = l.length := by
                calc
                  (l.toFinset.sum (fun e => l.count e) : ℕ) = (l.toFinset.sum (fun e => l.count e) : ℕ) := rfl
                  _ = l.length := by
                    classical
                    have h₅ : (l.toFinset.sum (fun e => l.count e) : ℕ) = l.length := by
                      rw [← Multiset.card_toMultiset]
                      simp [Multiset.sum_toFinset_count_eq_card]
                    exact h₅
              exact h₄
    have h₃ : (p.edges : List (Sym2 V)) = p.edges := rfl
    have h₄ : ∑ e : edgeSet p, (p.edges.count e : ℕ) = p.edges.length := by
      apply h₂
      <;> simp_all [edgeSet]
      <;> infer_instance
    calc
      ∑ e : edgeSet p, edgeCount p e = ∑ e : edgeSet p, (p.edges.count e : ℕ) := by
        simp [edgeCount]
        <;>
        simp_all [edgeSet]
        <;>
        rfl
      _ = p.edges.length := by
        apply h₄
      _ = (p.edges.length : ℕ) := by norm_cast
  calc
    ∑ e : edgeSet p, edgeCount p e = (p.edges.length : ℕ) := by
      exact h₁
    _ = p.length := by
      have h₂ : p.edges.length = p.length := by
        have h₃ : ∀ (p : G.LoopWalk u v), p.edges.length = p.length := by
          intro p
          induction p using LoopWalk.induction_on
          <;> simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length]
          <;>
          (try { contradiction })
          <;>
          (try {
            cases p <;> simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
          <;>
          (try {
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            simp_all [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length, List.map, List.cons, List.nil]
            <;>
            norm_num
            <;>
            aesop
          })
        exact h₃ p
      rw [h₂]
      <;> norm_cast
have h₁

have h₁ : ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e = p.length := by
    classical
    have h₂ : ∀ (l : List (Sym2 V)), ∑ e in l.toFinset, countP (· = e) l = l.length := by
      intro l
      have h₃ : ∑ e in l.toFinset, countP (· = e) l = ∑ e in l.toFinset, countP (· = e) l := rfl
      rw [h₃]
      -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
      have h₄ : ∑ e in l.toFinset, countP (· = e) l = ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e := by
        apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
        intro e _
        simp [countP_eq]
        <;> rfl
      rw [h₄]
      -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
      have h₅ : ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e = l.length := by
        calc
          _ = ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e := rfl
          _ = ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e := rfl
          _ = l.length := by
            -- Use the fact that the sum of counts of unique elements in a list is the length of the list
            have h₆ : ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e = l.length := by
              calc
                _ = ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e := rfl
                _ = ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e := rfl
                _ = l.length := by
                  -- Use the fact that the sum of counts of unique elements in a list is the length of the list
                  have h₇ : ∀ (l : List α), ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e = l.length := by
                    intro l
                    classical
                    -- Use the fact that the sum of counts of unique elements in a list is the length of the list
                    have h₈ : ∑ e in l.toFinset, l.count e = l.length := by
                      -- Use the fact that the sum of counts of unique elements in a list is the length of the list
                      rw [← Multiset.coe_card]
                      -- Use the fact that the sum of counts of unique elements in a list is the length of the list
                      simp [Multiset.countP_eq_card_filter, Finset.sum_const, nsmul_eq_mul, mul_one]
                      <;>
                      simp_all [List.countP, List.count]
                      <;>
                      rfl
                    exact h₈
                  exact h₇ l
            exact h₆
          _ = l.length := rfl
      rw [h₅]
      <;>
      simp_all [List.countP, List.count]
      <;>
      rfl
    -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
    have h₆ : ∑ e in p.edges.toFinset, countP (· = e) p.edges = p.edges.length := h₂ p.edges
    -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
    have h₇ : p.edgeSet = p.edges.toFinset := by
      simp [edgeSet]
    have h₈ : ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e = ∑ e in p.edges.toFinset, countP (· = e) p.edges := by
      calc
        _ = ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e := rfl
        _ = ∑ e in p.edgeSet, countP (· = e) p.edges := by
          apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
          intro e _
          simp [edgeCount]
          <;>
          rfl
        _ = ∑ e in p.edges.toFinset, countP (· = e) p.edges := by
          rw [h₇]
          <;>
          rfl
    -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
    have h₉ : ∑ e in p.edges.toFinset, countP (· = e) p.edges = p.edges.length := h₂ p.edges
    -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
    have h₁₀ : p.edges.length = p.length := by
      simp [edges, darts, LoopWalk.length]
      <;>
      rfl
    -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
    calc
      _ = ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e := rfl
      _ = ∑ e in p.edges.toFinset, countP (· = e) p.edges := h₈
      _ = p.edges.length := h₉
      _ = p.length := h₁₀
  -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
  have h₂ : ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e = p.length := h₁
  -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
  have h₃ : ∑ e : p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e = ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e := by
    simp [Finset.sum_const]
    <;>
    rfl
  -- Use the property of lists to show that the sum of counts of unique elements is the length of the list
  calc
    _ = ∑ e : p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e := rfl
    _ = ∑ e in p.edgeSet, p.edgeCount e := h₃
    _ = p.length := h₂
sum of `List

have h : ∑ (e : edgeSet p), edgeCount p e = ∑ e in (p.edges).toFinset, p.edges.count e := by sorry
  have h₂ : ∑ e in (p.edges).toFinset, p.edges.count e = p.edges.length := by sorry
  have h₃ : p.edges.length = p.length := by sorry
  sorry
